channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Honeywell Group invests in university infrastructure and leadership training for future-ready graduates
item.description: Honeywell Group, under the leadership of Oba Otudeko, don continue dey push education as one key way to build Nigeria economy strong. With plenty young Nigerians dey try enter university every year bu...

item.title: Supreme Court allows continued telehealth access to abortion pill amid legal dispute
item.description: On May 14, 2026, the U.S. Supreme Court temporarily blocked a lower court ruling that would have forced women to obtain the abortion pill Mifepristone through in-person visits. This decision allows wo...

item.title: Steyer Campaign Staffer Connected to Leaked Video of Katie Porter
item.description: A memo and reporting suggest that a current Tom Steyer campaign spokesperson was involved in filming and editing a 2021 U.S. Department of Energy Zoom call in which former Rep. Katie Porter was seen b...

item.title: Kenneth Okonkwo Criticises Obi and Kwankwaso for Leaving ADC for NDC
item.description: Kenneth Okonkwo, wey be member of the National Working Committee of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), don yarn strong criticism against former governors Peter Obi and Rabiu Kwankwaso after dem wa...

item.title: Ethiopia faces rising debt pressure despite new US and multilateral support
item.description: Ethiopia now has a public debt of $51.8 billion, which is putting serious strain on Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed’s plans for economic reforms. This comes at a time when the United States is preparing a f...

item.title: President Cyril Ramaphosa Faces Questions in Parliament on Security and Phala Phala Issue
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa appeared in the National Assembly on Thursday for a scheduled question-and-answer session. The session focused largely on the government’s recent deployment of the army in co...

item.title: Chinese President Xi urges partnership instead of rivalry with other nations
item.description: The full text of the article could not be accessed as the News Corp Australia website blocked the request, detecting the access attempt as a potential crawler bot. The provided information suggests th...

item.title: Planningtorock and Romy release a tender new version of ‘The One’
item.description: Electronic musician Planningtorock, also known as Jam Rostron, has collaborated with Romy from The xx to produce a fresh version of the 2011 track ‘The One’, originally featured on Planningtorock's se...

item.title: Co-operative Bank Posts Strong Q1 Results with Sh8.41bn Net Profit
item.description: Co-operative Bank of Kenya has reported a solid performance for the first quarter of 2026, with net profit rising by 21.3 percent to Sh8.41 billion, compared to the same period last year. This growth ...

item.title: Lucile Manguin: Iconic 1950s Fashion and Artistic Legacy
item.description: Lucile Manguin, born in 1899, was a pioneering French fashion designer and gallerist whose influence on mid-20th-century Parisian fashion remains notable. Operating her high-fashion house from 1928 to...

item.title: Exploring Life and Memory in Thokoza Through Photography
item.description: Sibusiso Bheka, a photographer born in 1997 in Thokoza, a township south of Johannesburg, captures the nuanced realities of South Africa's post-apartheid 'born-free' generation. His work explores the ...

item.title: Woman from Haryana murdered; body disposed in Himachal Pradesh
item.description: According to reports, a woman from Haryana was tragically murdered, and in an attempt to destroy evidence, her body was set ablaze in Himachal Pradesh. Authorities have registered a case and begun inv...

item.title: Tinubu urges fairness and unity in APC primaries, calls for inclusion of women and youth
item.description: President Bola Tinubu don advise All Progressives Congress (APC) leadership and all contestants wey dey their primaries make dem dey fair to everybody and avoid any do-or-die politics. Tinubu talk thi...

item.title: Supreme Court maintains access to mifepristone amid ongoing legal challenges
item.description: The United States Supreme Court has continued to block nationwide restrictions on the abortion drug mifepristone, following a conservative appeals court ruling that sought to halt mail-order and teleh...

item.title: Australia prepares specialised quarantine for hantavirus-exposed cruise passengers
item.description: Australia is preparing to quarantine six travellers and crew members repatriated from a hantavirus outbreak on the Dutch cruise-liner MV Hondius. The facility, located at the Bullsbrook Centre for Nat...

item.title: Toronto Al-Quds Day Organizers File Defamation Lawsuit Against Premier Doug Ford
item.description: The organizers of Toronto's annual Al-Quds Day rally have initiated a defamation lawsuit against Ontario Premier Doug Ford, alleging that his comments about the event were motivated by malice and raci...

item.title: Nigeria Pushes for Investment in Power and Mining Amid Ongoing Economic Reforms
item.description: Nigeria dey intensify effort to attract foreign and local investment into key sectors like power, mining, and infrastructure as part of the government's plan to boost economic growth and ensure sustai...

item.title: Paramount explores podcast deal with Katie Miller amid criticism
item.description: Paramount, a major media company with ties to former President Donald Trump, is reportedly in discussions to acquire or distribute 'The Katie Miller Podcast,' hosted by Katie Miller, the wife of White...

item.title: Brian Keith Hosaka appointed as commissioner of GCG
item.description: MANILA, Philippines — President Marcos has officially appointed Brian Keith Hosaka as a commissioner of the Governance Commission for Government-Owned and Controlled Corporations (GCG). Hosaka, who pr...

item.title: Court Hears that Gachagua's Impeachment Followed Proper Procedures
item.description: Lawyers representing Kenya's National Assembly have defended the impeachment of former Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua, telling the court that the process was carried out according to the constituti...
